mismatch_triage.justification: A_value and B_value have incompatible dimensions for integration.
License incompatibility does not necessarily block integration if usage complies with both licenses.
Semantic differences can be managed through careful mapping and transformation.
Version differences can be reconciled through configuration and parameter adjustments.
Purpose and pattern differences can be managed through orchestration and field mapping.
Temporal coverage mismatch can affect the integration of time-series data.

mismatches.root_cause: Incompatible dimensions between A and B
mismatches.why_it_blocks_integration: Different dimensions can lead to incompatible data structures and processing requirements.
mismatches.proposed_fix: Resample or interpolate data to match dimensions.

rules: Interpolate B_value to 2d spatial + 2d spectral frequency × direction
data_transform.example: Use linear interpolation to convert 3d data to 2d spatial + 2d spectral frequency × direction.

steps: Interpolate B_value to 2d spatial + 2d spectral frequency × direction

validation: Verify resampled data matches expected dimensions.

mismatches.root_cause: Incompatible temporal coverage between A and B
mismatches.why_it_blocks_integration: Different temporal coverage can lead to misaligned time-series data.
mismatches.proposed_fix: Resample or interpolate data to match temporal coverage.

rules: Resample A_value to multi-decadal to centennial
Interpolate B_value to days to decades
data_transform.example: Use linear interpolation to convert days to decades data to multi-decadal to centennial.

steps: Resample A_value to multi-decadal to centennial
Interpolate B_value to days to decades

validation: Verify resampled data matches expected temporal coverage.
